author: Dr. Sarah Chen
chiefComplaint: Worsening shortness of breath and holiday check-in
hpi:
69-year-old male with HFrEF (EF 30-35%), persistent A-fib on warfarin, CKD 3b, and Type 2 DM presents with 5 days of gradually worsening shortness of breath and mild chest pressure. He describes the dyspnea as an increase from his usual NYHA II baseline — previously able to walk to the mailbox without stopping, now needs to pause once halfway. He also notes that he was more short of breath climbing the 3 stairs to his front porch yesterday, which is usually manageable. He describes a vague chest tightness, non-radiating, rated 2/10, occurring with exertion and resolving within a few minutes of rest. No rest pain.

His wife Patricia reports he has been less adherent to his diet over the holiday season. They hosted a family Christmas dinner last weekend and ate leftovers for several days, with likely higher sodium content (ham, canned green beans, gravy). His weight this morning was 99.3 kg, up approximately 1 kg from his usual 98 kg baseline. She also notes a dry cough that started 2 days ago, worse when lying flat.

Reviewing his medication compliance: he has been taking all medications as prescribed, including the recently uptitrated carvedilol (now at 25mg BID as of late November per cardiology plan). He reports no dizziness on the higher carvedilol dose, though he feels slightly more fatigued than before. Home BP readings have been 128-136 systolic. His most recent labs from November showed stable renal function and potassium.

He is also requesting refills for his gabapentin, metformin, and vitamin D.
pastMedicalHistory:
1. Heart Failure with Reduced EF 30-35% (diagnosed 2019, last echo September 2020)
2. Type 2 DM with peripheral neuropathy (2010)
3. CKD Stage 3b (2018)
4. Atrial Fibrillation, persistent, on warfarin (2017)
5. CAD, 3-vessel, medically managed (2018)
6. HTN (2005)
7. Hyperlipidemia (2008)
8. BPH (2016)
9. History of gout (2019)
10. History of CAP (February 2021)
11. Vitamin D deficiency
12. Dyspepsia — under GI evaluation
medications:
1. Entresto 97/103mg BID
2. Carvedilol 25mg BID (target dose achieved November 2021)
3. Spironolactone 25mg daily
4. Furosemide 40mg BID
5. Warfarin 5mg daily
6. Insulin glargine 22 units QHS
7. Metformin 500mg BID
8. Atorvastatin 80mg daily
9. Gabapentin 300mg TID
10. Tamsulosin 0.4mg daily
11. Vitamin D3 2000 IU daily
12. Omeprazole 20mg daily (started by GI November 2021 for dyspepsia)
socialHistory: Former smoker, quit 1995 (20 pack-year). Rare alcohol. Retired accountant. Lives with wife Patricia. Active in church. Two adult children nearby. Family visited for holidays.
familyHistory: Father: MI at 62, HTN. Mother: T2DM. Brother: HTN. Sister: T2DM.

reviewOfSystems.Constitutional: Mild fatigue, slightly worse over past week — may be related to carvedilol uptitration or volume overload. No fevers, chills, or night sweats. Weight up ~1 kg from baseline.
reviewOfSystems.Cardiovascular: Worsening DOE and mild chest tightness with exertion as described. New dry cough, worse supine. No PND. Palpitations at baseline (A-fib). No syncope or presyncope. No peripheral edema worsening per patient (wife disagrees — see exam).
reviewOfSystems.Respiratory: Dry cough x 2 days, worse lying flat. No productive sputum, no hemoptysis, no wheezing.
reviewOfSystems.GI: Epigastric discomfort improved on omeprazole per GI. Appetite good (perhaps too good over holidays). No nausea, vomiting, or GI bleeding.
reviewOfSystems.GU: Nocturia x2-3 (slightly worse than usual). No dysuria or hematuria.
reviewOfSystems.Neurologic: Bilateral foot numbness/tingling at baseline. No dizziness despite carvedilol increase. No new weakness.
reviewOfSystems.Endocrine: Blood sugars running 130-170 fasting. Higher over holidays with dietary indiscretion.
reviewOfSystems.Psychiatric: Mood good — enjoyed family holiday gathering. Sleep disrupted by cough.

physicalExam.General: Alert, oriented, mildly fatigued-appearing but in no significant respiratory distress at rest. Can speak in full sentences. Obese.
physicalExam.HEENT: Normocephalic. PERRL. Oropharynx clear. JVP estimated at 8 cm H2O — mildly elevated, consistent with mild volume overload.
physicalExam.Cardiovascular: Irregularly irregular (A-fib), rate ~80 bpm. S1 normal, S2 present. Grade 2/6 holosystolic murmur at apex (known MR). No S3 gallop. No rubs. PMI laterally displaced.
physicalExam.Respiratory: Mild bibasilar crackles in lower one-third bilaterally, right = left. No wheezes or rhonchi. No accessory muscle use. Respiratory rate mildly elevated at 20.
physicalExam.Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly. No hepatojugular reflux.
physicalExam.Extremities: Trace to 1+ bilateral ankle edema — wife notes this is slightly more than his usual trace. Warm, well-perfused. No calf tenderness. DP pulses 1+ bilaterally.
physicalExam.Neurologic: Alert, oriented x4. Decreased sensation bilateral feet to mid-shin (stable). Motor intact. No asterixis.

assessment.diagnosis: Heart Failure with Reduced EF — mild volume overload
assessment.notes: Subacute worsening over 5 days in context of holiday dietary sodium indiscretion. Weight up ~1.3 kg from dry weight. Exam with mildly elevated JVP, bibasilar crackles, trace-1+ edema. Not severely decompensated — no hypoxia, no respiratory distress, SpO2 98%. BNP not checked today but clinical picture is mild congestion. This is a recurring pattern — this is the third episode of mild decompensation in 2021 (January, July, December), each triggered by dietary indiscretion. Now at target doses of neurohormonal blockade (Entresto + carvedilol 25mg BID + spironolactone). Furosemide 40mg BID may be chronically insufficient for this patient.
A1c 7.8% at last check (September). Holiday dietary indiscretion will likely worsen. Insulin glargine 22 units QHS. Home glucoses 130-170 fasting.
Cr 1.88, eGFR 38 per November labs. Stable trajectory. Now that carvedilol is at target, SGLT2i should be the next priority — will discuss with cardiology.
Rate 80 — slightly above target on carvedilol 25mg BID. Was 73 on submaximal dose. May be related to volume overload (sympathetic activation). Will see if improves with diuresis.

plan.action: Increase furosemide to 60mg BID for 5 days, then return to 40mg BID. Given this is the third episode in 2021, will discuss with cardiology whether a chronic increase to 60mg AM / 40mg PM (asymmetric) is warranted. Strict <2g sodium diet — wife understands holiday season is particularly challenging and will resume strict home cooking. Fluid restriction 1.5L/day. Daily weights — target return to 98 kg within 5 days. Return to ED if worsening dyspnea, orthopnea, SpO2 <92%, or weight increases despite increased diuretic.

Patient now at target dose (25mg BID) as of late November. BP 134/81 (well-controlled). HR 80 (slightly elevated — may improve with diuresis). Mild fatigue may be beta-blocker related vs. volume overload. Will reassess fatigue at next visit after euvolemia restored. Continue carvedilol 25mg BID — do not reduce dose.

A1c to be rechecked at next visit. Home glucoses slightly above target. Continue insulin glargine 22 units QHS. If A1c >8% at next check, will increase glargine to 24 units. Reinforce dietary counseling, particularly around the holiday season.
Labs to be drawn after the holiday — CMP, CBC in 2 weeks. Anticipate stable renal function but want to confirm after furosemide increase.
Gabapentin 300mg TID — refilled 90 days. Metformin 500mg BID — refilled 90 days. Vitamin D3 2000 IU daily — refilled 90 days. All other medications have adequate supply.
Phone follow-up in 5 days to check weight response to furosemide increase. Office visit in 6-8 weeks (early February). Labs in 2 weeks.

attestation:
I have personally evaluated this patient and agree with the documentation above.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Sarah Chen
Date: 12/24/2021
